observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to –
reconcile with the Municipal Accountant and determine the correct balance of gross receipts lodged in the Trust Fund and transfer the funds from the Trust Fund bank account to the General Fund bank account. The transfer of funds must be with duly approved Disbursement Voucher completely supported by working papers and approved Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for the transfer;
For succeeding collections, to record and deposit the resort collections in the General Fund cashbook and General Fund bank account, respectively. Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the collections and deposits in the General Fund.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to –
Create a Special Account in the General Fund for the operations of the resorts;
Make the necessary adjusting entries in the books once the collections from the resorts are transferred from the Trust Fund bank account to the General Fund bank account.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Transfer the receipts without specific purpose recorded in the Due to LGUs account of the Trust Fund totaling to ₱538,555.87 to the bank account of the General Fund. The transfer of funds must be with duly approved Disbursement Vouchers completely supported with verified working papers to be submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Follow the suggested accounting entries below for the disbursement voucher upon transfer of funds amounting to ₱538,555.87 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund:
Due to LGUs	xxx
Cash in Bank		xxx
prepare an accounting entry in the General Fund to record the receipt of funds from the Trust Fund to the General Fund amounting to ₱538,555.87 using the following suggested accounting entry:
Cash in Bank	xxx
Prior Period Adjustment	xxx
prepare an adjusting entry in the General Fund to transfer to the unappropriated surplus the balance in the Due to LGUs account amounting to ₱5,142.79 using the following suggested accounting entry:
Due to LGUs	xxx
Prior Period Adjustment	xxx
submit to the Office of the COA Auditor all the approved Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for the transfer and reversion of funds.
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to revert the balances totaling to ₱543,698.66 to the unappropriated surplus of the LGU and prepare a supplemental budget for its utilization;
For the subsequent period, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit receipts without specific purpose to the General Fund bank account and instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow proper accounting treatment of General Fund collections.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the balances in the Due to LGUs account and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances;

For subsequent transactions, instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review and approve the journal entry vouchers (JEVs) prepared by his bookkeepers to avoid misstatements.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to –
Follow the suggested accounting entries in recording the receipt of Share from NTA and the transfer of 20% to the LDF bank account, the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to be supported with bank snapshot with the amount credited to the bank account of the GF Proper;
Transfer monthly the 20% Share of NTA credited in the GF proper bank account to the LDF bank account; and
Adopt the Advice to Debit Account for the transfer of the 20% Share from NTA from the GF Proper bank account to the LDF bank account to avoid confusion and issuance of official receipt from LDF for the receipt of check issued from the GF Proper.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2021 | Management commented that the Local Finance Committee (LFC) in the beginning was hesitant in giving consent to carry out the resolution of the Sangguniang Bayan to disburse and release the said covid assistance the fact that no legal basis as to the disbursement of the same, but with the prodding of some, we move along a course where the resolution suggests wherein we have no other choice but to proceed in the releasing based solely on the approved resolution. It is timely that during the Exit Conference it was discussed and brought to us in the presence of some officials to let them know the repercussions of our blunder in giving such. As recommended from the Commission on Audit, the municipality through this representation would abide and definitely adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree no. 1445 particularly to Sections 2 & 4 and Section 305 (a) of Republic Act 7160 and other issuances from the Commission and discontinue granting similar assistance in the absence of any legal basis.
observations.reason: The LGU no longer granted COVID assistance without legal basis in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to exercise prudence in the utilization of government resources and observe the proper recording of transactions.
We also required the submission of the following documents in order to facilitate continuance of our audit and validate that the Municipality have met all the requirements warranting the grant of the CNA Incentive:
Resolution signed by both parties incorporating the guidelines/criteria for granting CNA Incentive;
Submit evidential documents to prove that the LGU generated savings from the allowable MOOE items enumerated under Item 4.3.1 of DBM Budget Circular 2020-5;
Certificate issued by the Head of Agency on the total amount of unencumbered savings generated from cost-cutting measures identified in the CNA which resulted from the joint efforts of labor and management and systems/productivity/income improvement
Submit accomplishment reports of PPAs programmed to be implemented for CY 2020 based on the approved budget, in compliance with Item 4.1.2 of the same DBM Budget Circular, in order to substantiate the grant of CNA Incentive to officials and employees; and
List of rank-and-file employees who are members of the employees' organization.
observations.reason: The Management commented that some documents were already submitted to the COA Office. Such documents are pending further audit and verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the LCE the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to exercise due diligence in the supervision over accountable officers under her control as mandated under Section 104 of P.D. 1445 and to review the reports made by them to ensure strict compliance with the guidelines in the remittance of the collections as required under Section 307 of RA 7160, for the protection of LGU funds; 
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review reports forwarded from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prior to recording to avoid errors in the financial reports; and
Direct the Liquidating Officer, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make reversal entry of the recycled deposit slip in the cashbook and in the Books of Accounts for proper presentation of the reports.
observations.reason: Collections are remitted almost daily
Total road network system is recorded in the books of the LGU. However, required reports were still not prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Accounting Office to undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the said payable accounts and effect necessary adjustments to revert the same to the appropriate account.
observations.reason: The interest payable was already dropped in the books of the LGU as of December 31, 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to conduct pre-repair and post-repair inspections to all repairs of motor vehicles and properties following the procedure enumerated in Chapter 5 of the Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System in order to ensure that the said repair and replacement of spare parts were necessary, done in accordance with the specification of the job order/contract and is 100% done. 

We further recommended that the results of the pre-repair and post-repair inspections, including the replacement of spare parts, must be written officially in the prescribed government forms and with complete information and data.

Furthermore, we recommended to the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain a subsidiary ledger to record the expenses related to repair and maintenance for each government vehicle and property owned by the LGU for monitoring purposes.

AAR 2019 | Management Comment per AAPSI 12/15/22:

The LGU is now integrating the repairs and maintenance records in to its new  information system, integration thru the GSO and MACCO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to implement the required property custodianship of inventories and supplies as prescribed under the Handbook on Property & Supply Management System and the Manual on New Government Accounting System with following courses of action:
Direct the General Services Officer to maintain complete records of stock cards and property cards.
Establish proper storage plan which shall include the designation of storage areas for each class of inventory or every program/project/ activity, guidelines on the proper storage of perishable goods, provision of pallets for piling of stocks, and designation of warehouse keeper who shall be authorized to open the storage facilities.
Strictly implement the use of Requisition and Issuance Slips to monitor the withdrawals of inventories.
observations.reason: Practice still existing in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create an inventory and disposal committee and to require the General Services Officer, through the LCE, to file an application for the disposal of unserviceable properties with the assigned auditor using the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP). Immediately conduct disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the Manual on Disposal of Government Property, to generate additional fund to the agency and avoid further deterioration and diminishing of market value.
observations.reason: Although the agency already created an inventory and disposal committee, no application for the disposal of unserviceable properties was filed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management effect full settlement of suspensions by submitting all the required documentations and explanations to avoid unnecessary complications arising from suspensions lapsing into disallowances.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation to the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned Offices to exert collaborative efforts for the realization of the cleansing of the PPE account of the LGU by undertaking the steps provided in the COA Circular 2020-006. Once the one-time cleansing is done instruct the –
Municipal General Services Office to:
Maintain an updated property cards per category of PPE;
Keep complete records of the PPE of the LGU;
Issue the necessary Property Acknowledgement Receipt once PPE is issued or assigned to an end-user; and
Undertake the annual physical inventory and reconcile with the records of Municipal Accountant’s Office.
Municipal Accountant’s Office to:
Maintain an updated Property, Plant and Equipment Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for Land; and
Record and disclose the PPE based on the recognition criteria and disclosure requirement set under IPSAS 17.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the Accounting Office to -
Verify each item recorded under the “Due to LGUs” and “Other Payables” accounts and reconcile these with the balances reflected in the General Ledger and if warranted, effect the necessary adjusting entries and submit the same to the Audit Team together with its supporting documents including the working papers; and
Submit detailed explanation to the Audit Team for all negative/abnormal balances.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management and the latter agreed to require the Municipal Accounting Office to-
Submit the related disbursement voucher for further review by the Audit Team;
Strictly observe the rules and regulations pertaining to the recording of the receipt of DRRMF from other LGUs as cash donations in the Trust Fund books using the account “Trust Liability–DRRMF”; and
Make the necessary adjustments to correct the entries made for the donations recorded in the “Due to LGUs” and “Trust Liability–DRRMF” accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to implement immediate actions to enforce liquidation of long outstanding cash advances which includes:
Continuous issuance of demand letters to the identified accountable officials/employees and submit to the Audit Team the duly received copies thereof.;
Implementation of salary deduction scheme with the consent of the concerned officials and employees to expedite return of unspent cash advances.; and
Commencement of collections measures to recover the long outstanding cash advances from persons liable who were already terminated form the government service or no longer connected with the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Expedite the evaluation of collectability of the receivable accounts and exert more efforts in the retrievable of supporting documents;
Issue demand letters to debtors/AOs that are still present and whose address or whereabouts can still be determined; and
Initiate the processing of request for authority to write-off the accounts which are uncertain for collection due to being dormant for more than 10 years or due to the death of the debtors/accountable officers.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer that the LGU funds should not be disbursed without approval by ensuring that the disbursement vouchers bear complete signatures of the authorized officials and written authority of LGU officials/employees who are authorized to sign in their absence.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned Barangay Governments and the Municipal Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents identified to enable complete audit evaluation of the transactions. We also recommended to require the Municipal Accounting Office to implement strict measures on examining and ensuring the completeness and sufficiency of the supporting documents that will establish the validity and propriety of disbursements in the purchases of lands to avoid suspension in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chef Executive to require that Bids and Awards Committee to implement public bidding for fuel contracts as provided in RA 9184 unless when the LGU has determined that Direct Retail Purchase is the best modality for the procurement of non-bulk petroleum fuel products. Such determined best mode of procurement should be properly documented and provided in the Annual Procurement Plan and the supporting documents for the payments of petroleum fuel products.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Stop paying in advance of complete deliveries to the suppliers and ensure proper documentation to prove completeness deliveries in compliance with Section 88(1) of PD 1445 and to review, and
Address the deficiencies in the deliveries by demanding the supplier to either refund the amount of deficiencies or to immediately deliver fuel amounting to ₱178,011.28.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to do the following:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to consolidate in the Annual Procurement Plan the items indicated in the Project Procurement Management Plan of all offices and to conduct the public bidding or alternative mode of procurement, if appropriate, to ensure that most advantageous prices to the LGU, and
In cases where reimbursement is warranted, require the Office Heads to avoid direct procurements of goods and services and strictly adhere to the procurement laws and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain individual ledgers of officials and employees and strictly monitor the payments remittances made and the corresponding deductions and payments of the employees, and
Require the concerned officials and employees for immediate refund of the overpayments made.
Ensure that similar situation will not occur in the future.

Recommendation:

We required the Municipal Budget Officer to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9344 and appropriate at least one percent (1%) of its IRA for the strengthening and implementation of PPAs of the LCPC and utilize the said allocation pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120.

Recommendation:

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee, through the BAC Secretary, to strictly submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof. In addition, ensure that the supporting documents are complete in compliance to RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit liquidation report to the Provincial Accounting Office with respect to the utilized amount; and
Debit the account “Due to LGUs” only upon the submission of liquidation report or settlement of liability.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management strictly comply with Administrative Order No. 263 in granting anniversary bonus. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant ensure that there is a legal basis in every allowance or bonus that is granted to LGU officials and employees.

• Require immediate refund of excess cash advances.
• Monitor overdue accounts. Send reminders in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-004 at their last known address for at least    three times. After exhausting all remedies, management may       request write-off of dormant accounts from the Commission on    Audit, with letters and tracers as supporting documents.
